title: բռնել
definition: to hold, to catch, to arrest, to occupy, to cover, to embrace
new_etymology: From Old Armenian բռնեմ (bṛnem, “to seize, to grasp”). Cognate with Middle Armenian and related to Proto-Indo-European *bher- (“to carry, to bear”).

title: բրոմ
new_etymology: Borrowed from French brome, from Ancient Greek βρῶμος (brômos, “stench”).

title: գայռ
new_etymology: Of unknown origin; possibly related to regional dialects.

title: գանգ
new_etymology: Inherited from Old Armenian գանգ (gang, “skull”). Cognate with Georgian გონგი (gongi).

title: գանձ
new_etymology: From Old Armenian գանձ (gandz, “treasure”), ultimately from Middle Persian گنج (ganj).

title: գառ
new_etymology: From Old Armenian գառ (gaṙ, “lamb”). Cognate with Georgian ღარი (ɣari).

title: գավակ
new_etymology: Of uncertain origin; possibly from regional dialects.

title: գեղմ
new_etymology: From Old Armenian գեղմ (gełm, “wool”). Cognate with Georgian ღილი (ɣili).

title: գեջ
new_etymology: Of uncertain origin; possibly from regional dialects.

title: գիտեմ
new_etymology: From Old Armenian գիտեմ (gitem, “I know”). From Proto-Indo-European *ǵneh₃- (“to know”).

title: գիրգ
new_etymology: Of uncertain origin; possibly from regional dialects.

title: գոգ
definition: lap, hollow, depression in the earth, hem (of clothes).

title: գուռ
definition: watering trough for people or animals.
new_etymology: Of uncertain origin; possibly from regional dialects.

title: գրել
new_etymology: From Old Armenian գրեմ (grem, “to write”). From Proto-Indo-European *gerbʰ- (“to scratch, to carve”).

title: գրենա
new_etymology: Of uncertain origin; possibly from regional dialects.

title: գրկել
new_etymology: From Old Armenian գիրկ (girkʿ, “bosom, lap”) + -ել (-el) verbal suffix.
